mnemonics: CIA: Clear, Initialize, Assign. The proper order for preparing a struct for use.
D.O.T.S: Declare struct, Obtain memory, Type members, Set values. A checklist for correct initialization.
keyPoints: Structure initialization is the process of assigning initial values to the members of a structure variable.
A structure can be initialized at the time of declaration using an initializer list, e.g., `struct Point p1 = {10, 20};`.
Members can also be initialized individually after declaration using the dot operator (`.`), e.g., `p1.x = 10; p1.y = 20;`.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to allocate memory for a pointer member within a struct before assigning a value to it, leading to a segmentation fault.
Mismatching the order or number of values in the initializer list with the order and number of members in the struct definition.
Assuming the compiler will automatically initialize all struct members to zero; local struct variables contain garbage values unless explicitly initialized.
